template.name: zabbig Linux Host
template.description: Host metrics collected by the zabbig_client monitoring agent.

item.name: App log: max API response time (ms)
item.description: Maximum response_time value seen in the access log since last scan.

trigger.name: Slow API response time on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: Maximum API response time exceeded 2000 ms in the last collection window.

item.name: App log: error detected
item.description: 1 = ERROR line appeared in the application log since last scan, 0 = clean.

trigger.name: Application error detected on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: An ERROR line was detected in the application log.

item.name: App log: payment API calls total
item.description: Cumulative count of POST /api/v1/payment lines in the access log.

item.name: App log: severity level
item.description: Highest severity in the application log since last scan. 3=FATAL, 2=ERROR, 1=WARN, 0=clean.

trigger.name: Application ERROR in log on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: An ERROR was detected in the application log.

trigger.name: Application FATAL error on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: A FATAL or OutOfMemory event was detected in the application log.

item.name: Load average 1 min
item.description: System load average over the last 1 minute.

trigger.name: High 1-min load average on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: 1-minute load average exceeds 10. Adjust threshold to match server core count.

item.name: Load average 5 min
item.description: System load average over the last 5 minutes.

item.name: Load average 15 min
item.description: System load average over the last 15 minutes.

item.name: CPU utilisation %
item.description: Total CPU utilisation across all cores (two /proc/stat reads, 200 ms apart).

trigger.name: Critical CPU utilisation on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: CPU utilisation has been above 95% for the last 5 minutes.

trigger.name: High CPU utilisation on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: CPU utilisation has been above 85% for the last 5 minutes.

item.name: Disk /data used %
item.description: Percentage of /data filesystem blocks in use.

item.name: Disk / free bytes
item.description: Bytes available to non-root users on the root filesystem.

item.name: Disk / inodes free
item.description: Number of free inode slots on the root filesystem.

item.name: Disk / inodes total
item.description: Total inode slots on the root filesystem.

item.name: Disk / inodes used
item.description: Number of inode slots in use on the root filesystem.

item.name: Disk / inodes used %
item.description: Percentage of inode slots in use on the root filesystem.

trigger.name: Inode exhaustion critical on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: Root filesystem inode usage above 95%.

trigger.name: Inode exhaustion warning on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: Root filesystem inode usage above 80%. Inode exhaustion prevents new file creation even when disk space is available.

item.name: Disk / used bytes
item.description: Bytes currently used on the root filesystem.

item.name: Disk / used %
item.description: Percentage of root filesystem blocks in use.

trigger.name: Disk / above 80% on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: Root filesystem is more than 80% full.

trigger.name: Disk / above 90% on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: Root filesystem is more than 90% full.

item.name: Memory available bytes
item.description: Bytes of RAM available for new allocations without swapping (MemAvailable).

item.name: Swap used %
item.description: Percentage of swap in use. Reports 0 when no swap is configured.

trigger.name: Swap in use on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: Swap usage has been above 20% for the last 5 minutes.

item.name: Memory used %
item.description: Percentage of RAM in use. Uses MemAvailable (not MemFree) for accuracy.

trigger.name: Critical memory usage on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: Memory usage has been above 95% for the last 5 minutes.

trigger.name: High memory usage on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: Memory usage has been above 85% for the last 5 minutes.

item.name: eth0 RX bytes/sec
item.description: Inbound throughput for eth0 in bytes/sec.

item.name: eth0 TX bytes/sec
item.description: Outbound throughput for eth0 in bytes/sec.

item.name: Network RX bytes/sec
item.description: Inbound throughput in bytes/sec across all non-loopback interfaces.

item.name: Network RX dropped
item.description: Cumulative receive packet drops since boot (kernel buffer overruns).

trigger.name: Network RX drops increasing on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: Receive drop counter increased by more than 100. May indicate kernel buffer overruns.

item.name: Network RX errors
item.description: Cumulative receive errors since boot across all non-loopback interfaces.

trigger.name: Network RX errors increasing on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: Receive error counter increased by more than 100 in the last collection cycle. May indicate NIC or cable issues.

item.name: TCP sockets in use
item.description: Number of currently open TCP sockets.

item.name: TCP orphaned sockets
item.description: Orphaned TCP sockets not attached to a file descriptor. Growing count indicates a socket leak.

item.name: TCP sockets TIME_WAIT
item.description: TCP sockets in TIME_WAIT state. Persistently high values indicate connection churn.

trigger.name: High TCP TIME_WAIT on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: TCP TIME_WAIT socket count has been above 5000 for 10 minutes. May indicate connection churn or port exhaustion.

item.name: Network TX bytes/sec
item.description: Outbound throughput in bytes/sec across all non-loopback interfaces.

item.name: Network TX dropped
item.description: Cumulative transmit packet drops since boot.

item.name: Network TX errors
item.description: Cumulative transmit errors since boot.

item.name: UDP sockets in use
item.description: Number of currently open UDP sockets.

item.name: Service: cron (process check)
item.description: 1 = cron process found in /proc, 0 = not found.

trigger.name: cron is not running on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: No cron process found. Scheduled jobs will not run.

item.name: Service: nginx
item.description: 1 = nginx active, 0 = not active.

trigger.name: nginx is down on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: nginx is not active.

item.name: Service: sshd
item.description: 1 = sshd active, 0 = not active.

trigger.name: sshd is down on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: sshd is not active. SSH access to the host may be unavailable.

item.name: System uptime
item.description: Seconds since last boot. A sudden drop indicates an unexpected reboot.

item.name: Probe: GitHub API body check
item.description: 1 = response body contains expected JSON key, 0 = otherwise. Checks https://api.github.com.

trigger.name: GitHub API body check failed on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: GitHub REST API response body does not contain expected JSON key. API may be returning an error page.

item.name: Probe: GitHub API body SSL cert
item.description: SSL certificate validity for api.github.com (body probe). 1=valid, 0=invalid, 2=unknown.

trigger.name: GitHub API body probe SSL cert invalid on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: TLS certificate for api.github.com is invalid (detected by body probe).

dependency.name: GitHub API SSL certificate invalid on {HOST.NAME}

item.name: Probe: GitHub API up (http_status)
item.description: 1=2xx, 2=4xx, 3=5xx, 0=unreachable. Checks https://api.github.com status code.

trigger.name: GitHub API not returning 2xx from {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: GitHub REST API at api.github.com is not returning a 2xx status code.

item.name: Probe: GitHub API response time (ms)
item.description: HTTP round-trip time to api.github.com in milliseconds. 0 when probe fails.

trigger.name: GitHub API slow response from {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: HTTP response time to api.github.com exceeded 3000 ms.

item.name: Probe: GitHub API SSL cert
item.description: SSL certificate validity for api.github.com. 1=valid, 0=invalid, 2=unknown.

trigger.name: GitHub API SSL certificate invalid on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: TLS certificate for api.github.com is invalid (expired, hostname mismatch, or untrusted CA).

trigger.name: GitHub API SSL certificate unknown on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: Could not verify TLS certificate for api.github.com. Host may be unreachable or using an unexpected TLS version.

dependency.name: GitHub API SSL certificate invalid on {HOST.NAME}

item.name: Probe: GitHub HTTPS port (github.com:443)
item.description: 1 = TCP port 443 on github.com is reachable, 0 = not reachable.

trigger.name: GitHub HTTPS port unreachable from {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: TCP port 443 on github.com is not reachable. Outbound HTTPS traffic may be blocked.

item.name: Probe: Google DNS (8.8.8.8:53)
item.description: 1 = TCP port 53 on 8.8.8.8 is reachable, 0 = not reachable. Basic outbound connectivity indicator.

trigger.name: Google DNS unreachable from {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: TCP port 53 on 8.8.8.8 is not reachable. May indicate a network outage or DNS filtering.

item.name: Probe: Google DNS response time (ms)
item.description: TCP round-trip time to 8.8.8.8:53 in milliseconds. 0 when probe fails.

item.name: Probe: httpbin JSON body check
item.description: 1 = https://httpbin.org/json contains expected JSON structure. Disabled by default.

item.name: Probe: httpbin JSON response time (ms)
item.description: HTTP round-trip time to httpbin.org/json in milliseconds. Disabled by default.

item.name: Probe: httpbin 200 check
item.description: 1 = https://httpbin.org/status/200 returns 2xx, 0 = otherwise. Disabled by default.

item.name: zabbig client: collectors failed
item.description: Number of collectors that failed or timed out in the last run.

trigger.name: zabbig client collectors failing on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: One or more collectors failed or timed out in the last run.

item.name: zabbig client: collectors total
item.description: Total number of enabled collectors in the last run.

item.name: zabbig client: run duration (ms)
item.description: Wall-clock duration of the last client run in milliseconds.

item.name: zabbig client: metrics sent
item.description: Total number of metric values successfully accepted by Zabbix in the last run.

item.name: zabbig client: run success
item.description: 1 = last run completed successfully, 0 = run ended with failures.

trigger.name: zabbig client not sending data on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: No data received from zabbig_client in the last 15 minutes. Client may have stopped or crashed.

trigger.name: zabbig client run failed on {HOST.NAME}
trigger.description: The last zabbig_client run ended with failures. Check the client logs.

field.name: ds.0.items.0
field.value: App log: max API response time (ms)
